name: 5-Minute Quick Warm-Up
description: A fast warm-up to get your voice ready. Start with lip trills to relax, then gentle scales to open up, and finish with a triad to engage your full voice.

exercises.notes: Start gentle. Let your lips buzz loosely.
Keep it light and easy. No pushing.
Open up into fuller tone now.

name: Full Warm-Up Routine
description: A comprehensive 10-minute warm-up that progresses from gentle to full voice. Perfect before rehearsals or performances.

exercises.notes: Start very gently. Wake up the voice.
Hold each note steady. Focus on breath support.
Smooth and connected. No breaks between notes.
Keep the same placement as you descend.
Open up to fuller volume.
Bridge through your passaggio smoothly.
Full voice. You should be warmed up now.

name: Performance Day Warm-Up
description: A gentle, voice-preserving warm-up for show days. Warms without fatiguing. Keep everything at comfortable volume.

exercises.notes: Very gentle. Just getting blood flowing.
Quiet to medium volume only. Save your voice.
Light and easy. Don't push.
Stay relaxed. You're ready.

name: Range Extension: High Notes
description: Gradually push your upper range with exercises that build from comfortable mid-voice up through your passaggio into head voice.

exercises.notes: Warm up first. Never push cold.
Establish your comfortable range.
Glide up smoothly. Feel the register shift.
Keep support engaged through the top note.
Don't reach for the top. Let it float.
Full range. Stay relaxed at the top.

name: Range Extension: Low Notes
description: Develop your chest voice and lower range. Focus on resonance and keeping the tone warm as you descend.

exercises.notes: Warm up gently first.
Let the voice settle down naturally.
Hold low notes. Focus on chest resonance.
Start from your lowest comfortable note.
The minor quality suits lower range work.

name: Agility & Runs
description: Build speed and precision with fast-moving patterns. Essential for pop, R&B, and musical theater singers who need to navigate melismatic passages.

exercises.notes: Warm up at moderate speed first.
Now double speed. Stay clean.
Every half step must be distinct.
Weaving pattern. Keep it smooth.
Think riffs. This is your improv vocabulary.
Push the tempo. How fast can you stay clean?

name: Breath Control Builder
description: Develop your breath support and sustaining power. These exercises focus on airflow management and dynamic control.

exercises.notes: Slow and steady. Feel the air support.
Hold as long as possible. Count your seconds.
Soft → loud → soft on one breath. The ultimate control exercise.
Very slow. One breath for the full pattern.
Try the full scale on one breath.

name: Belt Training
description: Build your belt voice with chest-dominant patterns in the mid-high range. Focus on power without strain.

exercises.notes: Warm up thoroughly. Belting cold = injury.
Establish your chest voice foundation.
Engage the diaphragm. Short, powerful bursts.
Power through the fifth. Keep it chest-dominant.
Mix as needed at the top but keep the power.
Full power triads. This is your belt zone.

name: Pop/Contemporary Warm-Up
description: A warm-up tailored for contemporary pop singing. Includes mix voice, agility, and pentatonic patterns common in pop music.

exercises.notes: Light and breezy. Think pop vocal tone.
Bright, forward placement.
The backbone of pop melody. Make it groove.
Rhythmic precision. Think vocal chops.
Smooth mix through the bridge.
Quick runs for ad-libs and riffs.

name: Classical / Musical Theater Prep
description: A warm-up focused on legato line, even tone, and the technical demands of classical and musical theater singing.

exercises.notes: Start with pure, steady tone. No vibrato yet.
Gentle connection through the range.
Dynamic control is everything in classical singing.
Even, legato line. Every note the same quality.
Practice the emotional color shift.
Seamless register transitions.
The 7th chord adds harmonic richness.

name: R&B Agility & Riffs
description: Build the vocal agility needed for R&B riffing and runs. Focuses on pentatonic patterns, chromatic movement, and speed.

exercises.notes: Warm up with the basics.
This IS the R&B scale. Own it.
Weaving thirds = instant riff vocabulary.
Chromatic slides for that smooth R&B feel.
Speed it up. Think Beyoncé runs.
Now pentatonic at riff speed. Go!

name: Beginner's First Week
description: A gentle introduction to vocal exercises for complete beginners. Simple patterns, moderate tempo, clear guidance. Do this plan daily for your first week.

exercises.notes: Bubble your lips loosely. This relaxes your voice.
Just hold one note. Focus on keeping it steady.
Do Re Mi Fa Sol and back. The foundation of everything.
Three notes of a chord. Listen to how they fit together.
Same as the 5-note scale but starting from the top.
